prompt: If I added 3 more CA1 tetrodes to my ripple detection electrode selection and re-populated, which downstream tables would change and which would be unaffected?

behavioral_checks: Identifies LFPBandV1 (or upstream LFP) as needing a new selection only if the new electrodes weren't already in the group
Identifies RippleTimesV1 as the table that re-populates
States that RippleParameters is UNAFFECTED because it is a parameter Lookup table, not electrode-dependent
Notes unrelated branches (decoding, position) are unaffected unless they consume the same ripple times

expected_output: Routes to ripple_pipeline.md and lfp_pipeline.md. Identifies what changes: a new RippleLFPSelection row (or modified one) flows into a new RippleTimesV1 entry. Notes that LFPBandV1 itself only needs to be re-populated if the new electrodes weren't already in the LFPBandElectrode part-table of LFPBandSelection (the precise gate — LFPBand has its own electrode part table that's a subset of LFPElectrodeGroup; if the new electrodes are already represented in LFPBandSelection's LFPBandElectrode rows, LFPBandV1 doesn't need re-population) (often they're in the LFP-band group already, so LFPBandV1 may not need to change). Identifies what is unaffected: position pipelines, decoding pipelines that don't directly depend on these specific ripple times, anything not downstream of RippleTimesV1. Mentions RippleParameters does not change (it's the parameter table, not electrode-dependent).
